name: R ORA enrichment rose
description: Display caller-supplied ORA pathway magnitude as radial bars, adjusted P as fill, Count as point size and optional direction/category as point outline.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Prefer the standardized output of ora-enrichment-adapter-r-v1.

parameters.name: top_n
parameters.description: Display limit after adjusted-P then magnitude ordering; it does not alter ORA results.

parameters.name: magnitude_label
parameters.description: Explicit label for the radial quantity.

parameters.name: direction_palette
parameters.description: Optional named colors for precomputed direction/category labels.

transformations.method: adjusted P ascending, magnitude descending, pathway label

supports_claims: Shows the relative magnitude, adjusted P and hit count of the supplied ORA rows, plus a caller-supplied direction/category when present.
does_not_support: Does not calculate ORA, prove pathway activation, create NES or a GSEA running score, resolve gene-set redundancy, establish mechanism, or imply causality.
required_statistics: definition of the magnitude and any direction field
preferred_companions: rectilinear dot plot for precise comparison
safer_alternatives: rectilinear ORA dot plot when exact magnitude comparisons are primary

visual_fingerprint.final_size_behavior: prefer double-column width and inspect labels/legends at final size
visual_fingerprint.review_note: Generalized code is distinct from the reviewed hiking-source image; exact recipe render review remains pending.

source.transformation: generalized_rewrite_with_explicit_ORA_adapter; removed hiking labels, scales, units and hard-coded annotations

notes: recipe.R parsed successfully under R 4.5.3 and the separate ORA adapter fixture passed.
Building the ggplot fixture terminated the local R process with Windows exit code -1073741819; execution/render are therefore not claimed.
The source hiking semantics remain in provenance only; the Recipe requires an explicit ORA adapter mapping.
No hiking-specific labels, units, axes or fixed annotations remain.
